papers.title: COEUR: a Canadian multisite ovarian cancer biobank
papers.institution: COEUR / CRCHUM / Canadian Cancer Trials Group
papers.access_route: Application to COEUR Scientific Committee; coordinated through CRCHUM Montreal; 12 contributing Canadian biobanks
papers.notes: n=1246 HGSC (high-grade serous carcinoma); FFPE + serum matched; ER/PR annotation available on subset; FIGO stage III dominates; 12 biobanks contributing; longitudinal serum at surgery and recurrence

papers.title: Washington University Gynecologic Oncology Tissue Bank: ovarian cancer specimens with matched plasma
papers.institution: Washington University in St. Louis Gynecologic Oncology Tissue Bank
papers.access_route: Collaborative research agreement with WashU GynOnc; IRB and MTA required
papers.notes: n=227 ovarian cancer; FFPE + plasma matched; ER annotation documented; Stage III/IV dominant; HER2 status available on subset; detailed treatment history (platinum, taxane) recorded

papers.title: Estrogen receptor expression in high-grade serous ovarian cancer: an HGSC TMAs study
papers.access_route: TMA slides available through consortium agreement; contact corresponding author
papers.notes: n=875 HGSC FFPE TMAs with ER/PR IHC scoring; no matched plasma; validates ER annotation approach; useful as annotation reference; FIGO stage III predominant

papers.title: NRG Biospecimen Bank (GOG legacy): ovarian cancer FFPE, plasma, and serum archive
papers.access_route: NRG Oncology correlative science committee application; nrgoncology.org/biospecimen-bank
papers.notes: >3.5 million specimens from GOG legacy trials; ovarian cancer dominant; FFPE + plasma + serum from randomised trial participants; ER/PR annotation from trial protocols; access requires NRG membership or collaboration

expected_retrievable_institutions: COEUR / CRCHUM / Canadian Cancer Trials Group
notes: COEUR (n=1246) and NRG (>3.5M specimens) are the two largest sources with ER annotation and matched FFPE + plasma. WashU provides a smaller but deeply annotated single-centre collection. The TMA paper (n=875) is ER annotation evidence but not a biobank access point.
